title: Dipteracanthus subulatus
taxonomy: Dipteracanthus subulatus is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This family, Acanthaceae, is a part of the larger clade asterids within the eudicots. The order Acanthales, to which Acanthaceae belongs, is a group of flowering plants that are primarily tropical and subtropical. Dipteracanthus subulatus is further classified as a member of the genus Dipteracanthus, which is a small group of plants within the family Acanthaceae. The classification of Dipteracanthus subulatus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Dipteracanthus subulatus, also known as Red Hot Cattip or Mexican Fire Cracker, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and subulate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Dipteracanthus subulatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dipteracanthus subulatus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves are also used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
